questions.question: Which of the following was the reason behind increase in manufacture of oil in the post-Mauryan period?

options.label: D
options.text: Oil was the center of economy

questions.explanation: In the post-Mauryan period ranging from 200 BC. to 300 A.D. the economy moved at an accelerated tempo. Manufacture of oil was also increased in the post-Mauryan period due to the use of the oil wheel.

questions.question: Satavahanas claimed their descent from which of the following varna?
questions.explanation: The Satavahanas claimed their descent from Brahamana varna. They performed Vedic rituals and worshipped gods like Krishna, Vasudeva, as evident in the Naneghat cave inscription of Naganika of 1st century BCE.

questions.question: Which of the following was the lowest level of administration during the Satavahana dynasty?
questions.explanation: The lowest level of administration during the Satavahana period was a grama or village. It was under the charge of a gaulmika or village headman. The village headman was also the head of a military regiment consisting of nine chariots, nine elephants, 25 horses and 45 foot soldiers.

questions.question: During which of the following period cotton was exported to different countries?
questions.explanation: Under the Satavahanas, agriculture was prominent. They produced and cultivated cotton. As mentioned in different foreign accounts, Andhra was famous for its cotton products also. Cotton was also exported from India to different countries.

questions.question: Which of the following refers to the royal share of merchandise brought into a town or harbour by merchants?
questions.explanation: Shulka during the Gupta period was a royal share of merchandise which was brought into a town or harbour by merchants. Therefore it can also be equated with the customs and tolls.

questions.question: Which of the following rock-cut caves at Ajanta are Chaitya Grihas?1) Cave number 92) Cave number 123) Cave number 19Select the correct option from the codes given below:
questions.explanation: The finest examples of the art during the Gupta period are Ajanta caves. Out of the 31 rock-cut cave monuments at Ajanta, five are Chaitya Grihas. The five chaitya grihas are cave numbers 9, 10, 19, 26, and 29.

questions.question: In which of the following ruling branches did the Vakataka kingdom divide after the death of Pravarasena?1) Nandivardhana2) VatsagulmaSelect the correct option from the codes given below:
questions.explanation: After the death of Pravarasena, Rudrasena I the son of Gautamiputra succeeded his grandfather Pravarsena to the throne and ruled from Nandivardhana, near modern Nagpur. Sarvasena, another son of Pravarasena, started ruling independently from Vastugulma.

questions.question: The largest number of Vakataka inscriptions belongs to which of the following kings?
questions.explanation: The largest number of Vakataka inscriptions belong to the reign of Pravarasena II. He was the son of Rudrasena II. A dozen copper-plate grants were discovered in different parts of Vidarbha.

questions.question: Which of the following ruled in southern Karnataka in 4th century A.D. and were contemporary of Pallavas?
questions.explanation: The Gangas were the contemporary of Pallavas. They ruled the southern Karnataka in 4th century A.D. Their Kingdom lay between the Pallavas in the east and the Kadambas in the west.

questions.question: The second branch of Kadambas formed after the death of Kakusthavarman, ruled from which of the following places?
questions.explanation: The Kadamba family split into two branches after the death of Kakusthavarman,. One of the branches continued to rule from the earlier capital Banavasi and the other branch ruled from Triparvata.
